title: Registering as unemployed (urząd pracy)
userSituation: I lost my job and want to understand registration at urząd pracy.
summary: What registering as unemployed means, how it differs from receiving unemployment benefit, what documents official sources list, and where foreigners' rights are described.
whoThisIsFor: People whose employment in Poland has ended
Foreigners checking what the labour office (urząd pracy) does
People who heard that registration can be connected with health insurance
commonDocuments: Identity document and residence document (for foreigners, a residence title such as a decision or residence card)
School certificates, diplomas, or qualification documents mentioned in official lists
whatThisExplains: The difference between unemployed status and unemployment benefit
Where the official registration information and document lists are
That foreigners' rights at the labour office depend on legal status and specific conditions
whatThisDoesNotDo: It does not confirm whether you can register in your situation
It does not decide anything about benefits
It does not fill in or submit the registration for you
It does not replace information from your local labour office
body:
Registering at the **poviat labour office** (*powiatowy urząd pracy*, often
just *urząd pracy*) is how a person without work enters the public employment
system. Registration gives you a formal status — **unemployed person** (*osoba
bezrobotna*) or **jobseeker** (*osoba poszukująca pracy*) — and access to
labour-market services such as job placement.

Two things are commonly confused:

- **Registering as unemployed** — the administrative status itself;
- **Unemployment benefit** (*zasiłek dla bezrobotnych*) — a payment with its
  own separate conditions. Registration does not automatically mean a benefit
  is paid; official sources describe the conditions separately.

For foreigners, official ministry pages explain that rights at the labour
office depend on **legal status** and specific conditions — and that after
registration, an unemployed foreigner may be covered by labour-market services
and **health insurance** on the same conditions as Polish citizens. Which
group you fall into depends on your residence basis, so the official pages and
your local office are the reference.

Official document lists for registration mention an identity document, a
residence title for foreigners (for example a decision or residence card),
employment certificates, and qualification documents. Registration is
typically handled at the labour office for where you live, and parts of it can
be started online through praca.gov.pl.
